intro: Hanburia oerstedii, commonly known as "Miercillo," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hanburia oerstedii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hanburia oerstedii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from fever, pain, and digestive discomfort.
